meta.title: kurl · URL shortener with click analytics
meta.description: Shorten long URLs. Analyze clicks by device, channel, and country.

home.subhead: See who clicked from where — by device, OS, browser, channel, country.
home.anonymousHint: No login required. Stats need login though.

stats.title: Analytics dashboard
stats.desc: Human/bot split, channel, country, time-of-day — automatic. 30-day trend + heatmap.

viral.title: Share OG card
viral.desc: Share on KakaoTalk/X and the preview shows the live click count baked in.

profile.title: Public profile
profile.desc: kurl.me/u/{your-id} — the one link for your Instagram bio.

domain.title: Your own domain
domain.desc: Route through go.brand.com. TXT + CNAME auto-verified.

home.loginCta: Sign in to save and analyze <clickStats>click stats</clickStats> for this link.

realtime.title: Live click feed (SSE)
realtime.desc: Watch clicks arrive in real time on the stats page — time / country / channel / device, streaming

abtest.title: A/B split + geo-routing
abtest.desc: One short URL → weighted variants and per-country destinations (up to 4 per link)

insights.title: Automatic insights
insights.desc: Peak hour, return rate, half-life, velocity — surfaced without any setup

webhooks.title: HMAC-signed webhooks
webhooks.desc: Signed JSON POST per click to Slack / your endpoint. Skip bots / sample / batch / quota cuts cost up to 98%

safety.title: Layered abuse defence
safety.desc: Google Safe Browsing + anonymous-shorten PoW + datacenter ASN auto-bot classification

twofa.title: 2FA TOTP + recovery codes
twofa.desc: Pair an authenticator app and add a 6-digit step to sign-in. 10 single-use codes, BCrypt-hashed

home.recentSubhead: Kept on this browser for 24 hours. Sign in to keep them forever.
home.statsSubhead: Total links shortened and clicks tracked so far.
home.featuresSubhead: Live click feed · A/B + geo routing · HMAC webhooks · 2FA · auto-insights — everything you need to run links.
home.whyTitle: Why don't other shorteners ship this?
home.whySubhead: Features usually gated behind enterprise tiers — free here, on by default.

errors.invalid: URL must start with http:// or https://.

errors.customCodeFormat: Custom code must be 3-16 letters or digits only.
errors.urlScheme: URL must start with http:// or https://.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Sign in to manage your links and view analytics.
login.anonymousAlt: You can also <shorten>shorten URLs</shorten> without signing in.

twofa.desc: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
twofa.descRecovery: Enter one of your recovery codes. Each code can only be used once.
twofa.toggleToRecovery: Lost your authenticator? Use a recovery code

dashboard.label: dashboard
dashboard.title: My Links
dashboard.subtitle: {count} links. Click a short code to see analytics.
dashboard.searchPlaceholder: Search by original URL or short code
dashboard.loginRequiredDesc: My links and click stats are visible only after sign-in.
dashboard.emptyDesc: Shorten one URL and within a minute the clicks / countries / channels / devices view fills in. Takes 30 seconds.
dashboard.noResultDesc: Could not find any link matching "{query}".
dashboard.deleteConfirmDesc: Deleted links cannot be recovered. Click stats are also lost.
dashboard.bulkSelectAll: Select all links on this page
dashboard.bulkDeleteConfirmDesc: {count} links and all their click stats will be permanently removed.

bulkImport.title: Bulk import from CSV
bulkImport.description: Header columns: url (required), custom_code, expires_at (ISO-8601). The first row is auto-detected as a header if it matches.
bulkImport.limitHint: Up to 100 rows per upload. You'll get a result CSV after validation.
bulkImport.resultHint: The result CSV includes per-row outcome (short_url or error).

edit.title: Edit link
edit.expiresAt: Expires at (leave blank for no expiry)
edit.notePlaceholder: e.g. Spring promo / used in Instagram story
edit.noteHint: Visible only to you. Never shown to visitors.
edit.expiredMessagePlaceholder: e.g. Promo ended — see the next drop at example.com
edit.expiredMessageHint: Shown instead of the default 'gone' page when the link expires or hits its view limit.
edit.invalidUrl: URL must start with http:// or https://.
edit.maliciousUrl: URL flagged as malicious or phishing.

tags.description: Type tags for this link. New names are created automatically.

og.description: Override the preview shown when this link is pasted on KakaoTalk, Slack, Twitter, etc. Leave fields blank to use the auto-scraped values from the destination page.
og.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g., One-click URL shortener with click analytics

protection.description: Set a password and a maximum view count to protect this link.
protection.passwordSetHint: Password already set (type a new one to change)
protection.maxViewsInvalid: Max views must be a positive integer.

descriptions.overview: The essentials — click quality, live feed, day×hour heatmap.
descriptions.traffic: Volume shape — daily trend, hour-of-day, country split.
descriptions.sources: Where it came from — referrers, UTM (4), channel/src tags.
descriptions.audience: Who came — device, OS, browser, region, language, bots.
descriptions.settings: Per-link config — A/B destinations, webhook delivery.

live.title: Live clicks
live.waiting: Clicks will appear here as they come in.

quality.title: Click quality
quality.peakHourHint: Most clicks land in this hour

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Each click triggers an HMAC-signed JSON POST to the URL you register. Wire it into Slack/Discord incoming webhooks or your own analytics endpoint. Up to 5 per link.
webhooks.issuedHint: This secret is shown only once. Use it on the receiver to verify the X-Kurl-Signature (sha256=hex) header.
webhooks.optBatch: Batch mode (one POST every 5s, max 50 events)

destinations.title: A/B split / geo-targeting
destinations.description: Distribute clicks across destinations by weight, or send visitors from specific countries to a tailored page. Country-tagged variants only fire for matching visitors; otherwise traffic falls back to country-agnostic variants, then the original URL. Up to 4 per link.
destinations.empty: No destinations yet. Add some to start splitting traffic by weight.

stats.loginRequiredDesc: Stats are visible to the link owner only.
stats.notFoundDesc: It may have been deleted or never existed.

srcChannel.title: Share channel (?src)
srcChannel.desc: Tracked via the ?src= hint on the short URL itself — covers QR scans, KakaoTalk, offline channels where the referrer header is missing.

asn.title: Network / ASN
asn.desc: Autonomous system (ISP / cloud provider) distribution. {dc} datacenter clicks were auto-flagged as bots.

redirectPerf.title: Redirect performance
redirectPerf.desc: Short-URL → destination latency. Cumulative since this instance started.

activeUsers.title: Active users (DAU / WAU / MAU)
activeUsers.desc: Distinct users active in the selected window.

lifecycle.title: Link lifecycle
lifecycle.desc: Click distribution by day-since-link-creation, up to ~30 days.

cohort.title: Cohort retention
cohort.desc: % of each weekly signup cohort active in subsequent weeks. Darker = higher retention.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The address may be wrong, the short link may have expired, or the page may not exist.

qr.title: qr code
qr.logoLabel: Drop the destination favicon in the center

demo.lead: The numbers below are seeded for the demo, but the layout fills in for real on your own links — bot/human split, UTM channels, geo, time-of-day, all automatic.

daily.desc: Last 30 days — weekday/weekend pattern + campaign spikes at a glance.

source.title: Channels (utm_source)
source.desc: Instagram bio, KakaoTalk shares, QR scans — auto-tagged on every click.

demo.footerLead: Your real link fills in this layout within 60 seconds of the first click.

weeklyInsights.emptyDesc: No clicks yet this week. Try tagging channels with UTM to start filling this in.

recent.claimCta: Sign up to keep these links permanently in your account.
recent.anonymousNote: Anonymous links auto-expire after 24h. Sign in and they move into your account permanently — no expiration.

statsEmpty.title: No clicks yet
statsEmpty.description: Share this short link on KakaoTalk / Slack / social — analytics will start the moment someone clicks.

whyKurl.disclaimer: Compared against bit.ly / short.io / rebrandly / TinyURL free tiers (2026).

abtest.label: A/B split (weighted routing)
abtest.note: One short URL → multiple destinations by weight. Usually enterprise-only.

geoRouting.label: Per-country destinations
geoRouting.note: Auto-route visitors to a page tailored to their country. Almost no free shortener offers this.

webhooks.label: HMAC-signed webhooks
webhooks.note: SHA-256 signed POST per click + cost controls (batch / sample / quota).

realtime.label: Live click feed (SSE)
realtime.note: Watch clicks arrive on the stats page in real time. Rare in this category.

insights.label: Automatic insights
insights.note: Peak hour, return rate, half-life, velocity — surfaced without setup.

asnTracking.label: ASN / datacenter IP classification
asnTracking.note: Auto-flags cloud egress (AWS/GCP/Azure) as bots — kurl-specific.

botDefense.label: Layered bot defence
botDefense.note: Safe Browsing + anonymous-shorten PoW + ASN auto-classification.

twofa.label: 2FA TOTP + recovery codes
twofa.note: Google Authenticator + 10 single-use recovery codes. Often paid elsewhere.

about.title: kurl — URL shortener with click analytics
about.lead: Shrink long URLs and see who clicks from where. Free, no ads.
about.section1Body: Most shorteners hide analytics behind a paywall. kurl gives you device, OS, browser, channel, country, and hour-of-day breakdowns for free.
about.section2Body: Each visit is filtered for bots and malicious URLs, then redirected. Clicks are denormalized into wide analytic columns so charts load fast even at scale.
about.section3Body: Spring Boot · MySQL · Redis · Next.js 14 · AWS ECS Fargate. Source on GitHub.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.lead: Free covers shortening + full analytics. Pro adds custom-domain auto-TLS, unlimited links, permanent stats.
pricing.freeFeature2: 8 click-analytics dimensions (time / device / country / channel / OS / browser / UTM / bot)
pricing.freeFeature4: A/B split + geo routing (4 destinations per link)
pricing.rightsLead: GDPR / privacy-law guarantees — available to every user regardless of plan.
pricing.right3: Only one cookie (sign-in) — no analytics or ad cookies

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.p1: These terms govern your use of kurl. Using the service means you accept them.
terms.p2: kurl is for shortening lawful URLs only. Shortening is rejected or revoked for: malicious/phishing destinations, spam, copyright violations, hate content, doxxing.
terms.p3: Public http/https URLs only. Private IPs, localhost, file:// and similar are blocked.
terms.p4: We screen with Google Safe Browsing plus custom bot heuristics. Abusive accounts may be suspended without notice.
terms.p5: Service provided AS-IS without availability guarantees. Operators are not liable for data loss or downtime.
terms.p6: Terms may change with notice via the "Last updated" timestamp and, where possible, email.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: kurl collects the minimum data needed and anonymizes by default. This policy follows GDPR Article 13 and Korea's PIPA.
privacy.p2: OAuth sign-in: email, provider (Google), signup date. Shortening: original URL, short code, expiry. Clicks: timestamp, User-Agent, Referer, IP with last octet masked, GeoIP-derived country/region/city.
privacy.p3: Service operation (redirect, stats), abuse protection, security incident response. Never sold or used for marketing.
privacy.p4: Anonymous links auto-expire after 24h. Authenticated links persist until you delete. Expired links + their clicks are auto-cleaned 30 days after expiry. Account deletion erases everything immediately.
privacy.p5: Stored IPs are masked (IPv4 last octet / IPv6 beyond first two groups). Raw IPs exist transiently in memory and logs only — never written to the database.
privacy.p6: GDPR Article 17 (erasure) and Article 20 (portability) honored. Settings page exposes data download (JSON) and permanent account deletion.
privacy.p7: Google OAuth (email, name), Google Safe Browsing API (URL hashes for malware checks), MaxMind GeoLite2 (offline GeoIP — no outbound calls).
privacy.p8: One HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict cookie for the JWT refresh token. No analytics or ad cookies.
privacy.p9: Data inquiries: GitHub issues or the operator's email.

settings.title: Settings

descriptions.account: Email / joined date / timezone / UI language.
descriptions.security: 2FA TOTP + recovery codes / API key issue & revoke.
descriptions.domains: Route short URLs through your own domain (go.brand.com).

profile.title: Public profile
profile.intro: Bundle your short links into one shareable page — perfect for an SNS bio.
profile.usernameHint: 3–16 lowercase letters / digits / underscore. Choose once, can't change later.
profile.bioPlaceholder: e.g. fullstack dev / building side projects
profile.featuredEmpty: Add a link from below to feature it.

settings.timezoneHint: Used as the report time zone on stats pages.
settings.languageHint: You can also change this from the language switcher in the header.
settings.exportHint: User info + all links + click events (IPs are masked).
settings.deleteHint: Your account enters a 30-day grace period after deletion. Sign back in within that window to restore everything; after 30 days the account and all links/stats are permanently removed. Anonymous links are not affected.
settings.deleteConfirmDesc: Sign back in within 30 days to restore. After that this is permanent. Type "DELETE" to confirm.

twofa.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
twofa.description: Add a TOTP authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.) for an extra step at sign-in. Save the 10 recovery codes shown right after enabling — they're your backup if you lose access to the app.
twofa.scanTitle: Scan the QR with your authenticator
twofa.scanHint: Or type the manual key into your app if scanning isn't possible.
twofa.confirmRegenerateHint: Enter a current code to issue 10 fresh recovery codes. Existing codes will stop working.
twofa.confirmDisableHint: Enter a current code or recovery code to turn 2FA off.
twofa.recoveryTitle: Save your recovery codes somewhere safe
twofa.recoveryHint: Each code works only once. Use them if you lose access to your authenticator app — print them or store in a password manager.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.description: Use these from scripts or automations that call the kurl API. The full key is shown once — copy it somewhere safe immediately.
apiKeys.justIssuedHint: You won't be able to see this again. Store it like a password.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoking this key disables it immediately. Continue?

customDomains.title: Custom domains
customDomains.description: Serve short links from your own domain (e.g. go.brand.com). Add TXT + CNAME records — we auto-verify in the background (polls DNS for up to 10 min). Easiest TLS path: front your domain with Cloudflare free plan.
customDomains.empty: No domains yet. Enter your domain above to start.
customDomains.verifyFailed: TXT record not found. DNS may take 1–5 min to propagate.
customDomains.deleteConfirm: Remove this domain? Short URLs on this domain will stop working immediately.
customDomains.dnsTxtHint: Ownership check — add this TXT record at your DNS provider
customDomains.dnsCnameHint: Traffic routing — add this CNAME record at the same provider

homeFaq.title: Frequently asked questions
homeFaq.a1: Anonymous links auto-expire after 24h. Links you make signed in last until you delete them.
homeFaq.q2: Can I use it without signing in?
homeFaq.a2: Yes — shortening works without an account. Stats and permanent storage need Google sign-in.
homeFaq.a3: On the My Links page click any short code to open the 8-dimension stats page.
homeFaq.q4: Will previews show in KakaoTalk / Slack?
homeFaq.a4: Yes. kurl detects social bot User-Agents and serves an OG-tagged HTML page instead of a redirect, so previews render correctly.
homeFaq.a5: We screen with Google Safe Browsing at shorten time. Flagged URLs are rejected.
homeFaq.a6: Yes — shortening is one POST to /api/v1/links. For authenticated endpoints, issue a key under Settings > API keys. (The full OpenAPI doc is gated to operators.)
